Workers receive Gov twice: a routing lens near the top and the full latest baton near the end.
Source context is ground truth. The state brief preserves continuity. The final baton controls the next action.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

treating product family ECCN match as sufficient for release
using policy statement as license substitute
assuming end-user alignment compensates for destination mismatch

dependency_ledger: License scope (Peru) ⊥ Shipment destination (Chile) | Unresolvable within current corpus | Requires external evidence or amended license | Blocks closure
full_latest_gov_baton.final_compiler_directive: FAIL_CLOSED. Destination mismatch is terminal. License LIC-SIE-073 explicitly authorizes Peru. Shipment SHIP-SIE-073 routes to Chile. Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates exact match on destination. No source record bridges this gap. No repair path exists. Advise human compliance officer to either (a) obtain license amendment covering Chile, or (b) reroute shipment to Peru under LIC-SIE-073, or (c) cancel shipment. Do not release.
must_preserve: Advisory status of warning (WARN-SIE-073 cannot authorize)

next_worker_baton.attack_focus: All artifacts pass deterministic gate. Both workers independently identify identical open blocker: destination mismatch between license (Peru) and shipment (Chile). No repair path exists within source corpus. No bridge source identified. Continued iteration offers no value.
monotonic_preservation: Advisory status of warning (WARN-SIE-073 cannot authorize)

full_latest_gov_baton.rationale: Both SOURCE_BOUNDARY_MAPPER and ADVERSARIAL_SCOPE_CHALLENGER independently reach identical ESCALATE verdict with identical open blocker. Deterministic gates pass on both artifacts. No adversarial repair or scope challenge produces new evidence. Destination field mismatch is binary and unbridgeable from current source corpus. Gov governance rule terminates when no repair path exists and continuation would violate monotonic preservation. FINAL_COMPILER authority invoked.

worker_output.final_answer: ESCALATE. Shipment SHIP-SIE-073 to Chile fails exact match with LIC-SIE-073 destination Peru. WARN-SIE-073 reinforces need for precise license evidence. No source record closes the boundary for release.

worker_output.final_answer: ESCALATE. The shipment to Chile (SHIP-SIE-073) fundamentally mismatches the license destination of Peru (LIC-SIE-073). Policy STD-SIE-073-P mandates an exact match across all fields, including destination. The warning WARN-SIE-073 reinforces the need for precise license evidence, not broad assumptions. No source record provides a valid bridge to reconcile this destination discrepancy, thus the action boundary remains open.
open_blockers: Destination mismatch between license scope (Peru) and shipment destination (Chile).
No source record bridges the gap between Peru-licensed and Chile-intended shipment.
General warning WARN-SIE-073 cannot override explicit license scope.

internal_documents.content: Shipment SHIP-SIE-073 exports ECCN 3A991 sensor array to Chile for end user Andes Labs on 2026-06-28.
Dual-use warning WARN-SIE-073 says similar sensor arrays require exact license evidence and should not rely on product family alone.
Export license LIC-SIE-073 covers ECCN 3A991 sensor array, destination Peru, end user Andes Labs, shipment date 2026-06-28, effective before export.

policy_documents.content: Controlled export is complete only when export license evidence matches ECCN, destination, end user, shipment date, and license effective date before export.
